message:
feat(socket): port 6 remaining glibc reserved socket aliases (bd-1opky)

Completes the underscored-alias coverage for the socket entry
points (started in bd-xniy3) by adding the remaining six:

- `__socketpair`, `__sendmsg`, `__recvmsg`, `__setsockopt`,
  `__getsockopt`, `__shutdown`

glibc emits these as both internal-use entry points and as the
symbols statically-linked NSS modules and libc-internal helpers
link against. Without them callers see undefined-reference
errors when linking statically.

Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers
delegating to the public counterparts in socket_abi — same
pattern as bd-xniy3's __accept/__bind/__listen/__sendto/
__recvfrom/__getsockname/__getpeername.

Tests: 4 abi integration tests covering: __socketpair creates a
connected pair; __sendmsg/__recvmsg round-trip via msghdr+iovec
on a SOCK_DGRAM socketpair; __setsockopt+__getsockopt SO_KEEPALIVE
round-trip on an IPv4 TCP socket; __shutdown SHUT_RDWR on a
socketpair returns 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1nvd","title":"syscall: renameat2 invalid flags should override both-null path EFAULT with EINVAL","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for renameat2(AT_FDCWD, NULL, AT_FDCWD, NULL, 0x80000000). Add ABI regression coverage so bad flags stay dominant over null-path faults.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:21:23.970875104Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T01:01:36.481669880Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T01:01:36.478993356Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1o4k","title":"Verification matrix maintenance: backfill rows for all open critique beads + restore dashboard stats","description":"Background:
- scripts/check_verification_matrix.sh and harness integration tests enforce that every open/in_progress critique bead has a verification_matrix row and that dashboard stats are internally consistent.
- New critique beads have been added since bd-id3 closed, and verification_matrix.json dashboard regressed (missing by_obligation_type).

Scope:
- Ensure tests/conformance/verification_matrix.json contains entries for every open/in_progress critique bead in .beads/issues.jsonl.
- Restore dashboard.by_obligation_type and make dashboard totals/breakdowns consistent with entries.
- Keep changes deterministic and minimal-diff (stable ordering by bead_id).

Deliverables:
1) Updated tests/conformance/verification_matrix.json with missing rows backfilled.
2) Updated dashboard fields: total_critique_beads, total_entries, by_coverage_status, by_priority (with complete/partial/missing), by_obligation_type, by_stream.
3) Optional: add a tiny sync helper script if needed for determinism (but keep CI gates authoritative).

Acceptance:
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness passes (including verification_matrix_test + matrix_drift_test).
- bash scripts/check_verification_matrix.sh passes.
- bash scripts/check_matrix_drift.sh passes (0 missing rows).

Notes:
- Do not delete stale rows for closed beads; closure gate relies on matrix rows for closed critique beads.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RusticCastle","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:38:53.805640595Z","created_by":"RusticCastle","updated_at":"2026-02-12T21:59:21.600904898Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T21:59:21.600885191Z","close_reason":"Added sync helper + verified verification-matrix gates and harness tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","critique","verification"],"comments":[{"id":684,"issue_id":"bd-1o4k","author":"RusticCastle","text":"## Verification Matrix Maintenance (bd-1o4k)

  - Recomputes dashboard stats (including `by_obligation_type`)

Verified
- `python3 scripts/sync_verification_matrix.py --check`
- `bash scripts/check_verification_matrix.sh`
- `bash scripts/check_matrix_drift.sh`
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness`

Note
- Agent Mail MCP tool calls are currently timing out in this environment, so coordination is via bead comments for now.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:59:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1om","title":"Perf: Golden snapshot outputs + sha256 regression gate","description":"Capture golden outputs for runtime_math telemetry so behavior drift is detectable.

Acceptance criteria:
- Produce one or more canonical snapshot JSONs under tests/ (or harness output fixtures).
- Store sha256 checksums; verify in CI/local gate.
- Golden set covers strict and hardened modes.

Rationale:
- We need isomorphism proofs for behavior changes; golden outputs are the cheap continuous check.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltForge","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:56.662031099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T05:46:29.172839801Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T05:46:29.172817930Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":685,"issue_id":"bd-1om","author":"CobaltForge","text":"Implemented golden runtime_math snapshot fixtures + sha256 regression gate.

- Added canonical golden snapshot fixture: `tests/runtime_math/golden/kernel_snapshot_smoke.v1.json` (covers strict + hardened via mode=both).
- Added checksum file: `tests/runtime_math/golden/sha256sums.txt` (format compatible with `sha256sum -c`).
- Added gate script: `scripts/snapshot_gate.sh`
  - Regenerates snapshot into `target/runtime_math_golden/` and validates against committed sha256sums.
  - On mismatch, prints a truncated diff vs the golden fixture.
- Added update script: `scripts/update_golden_snapshots.sh` to intentionally refresh the committed golden + sha256sums when behavior changes with an isomorphism proof.
- Wired into CI: `scripts/ci.sh` now runs `scripts/snapshot_gate.sh`.

Verification:
- `scripts/snapshot_gate.sh` passes locally (stable output / sha256 match).","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:46:29Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1oz","title":"EPIC: Fuzzing Infrastructure Completion","description":"Goal: Comprehensive fuzzing infrastructure to discover edge cases and vulnerabilities.

Background:
frankenlibc-fuzz crate exists but needs completion. Fuzzing should cover:
1. All ABI entrypoints with arbitrary C inputs
2. Membrane validation pipeline edge cases
3. Runtime math kernel state transitions
4. Allocator edge cases (size classes, alignment)

Fuzz Targets by Priority:
1. String operations (memcpy, strcpy, sprintf, etc.) - highest attack surface
2. Allocator (malloc/free/realloc sequences)
3. Format strings (printf family)
4. Resolver/network (DNS parsing, socket operations)
5. Locale/encoding (iconv, collation)

Infrastructure:
- cargo-fuzz integration (libFuzzer)
- OSS-Fuzz compatibility
- Corpus management and minimization
- Coverage-guided with structure-aware mutations
- CI integration with nightly fuzzing runs

Success Criteria:
- Zero crashes after 10B iterations per target
- All healing paths exercised and verified
- Coverage reaches 80%+ of membrane code
- No hangs or infinite loops discovered

Verification:
- Fuzz artifacts stored and reproducible
- Crash reports include TSM state snapshot
- Healing action distribution logged","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:58:03.059535087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T22:52:26.862440662Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T22:52:26.862222368Z","close_reason":"All children closed. Fuzzing Infrastructure: frankenlibc-fuzz crate with targets for string ops, allocator sequences, format strings. CI integration operational.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzzing","security","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1oz","depends_on_id":"bd-1m5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1oz","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":686,"issue_id":"bd-1oz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1oz.1","title":"Fuzz target: string operations","description":"Fuzz string operations for edge cases.

Target Functions:
- memcpy, memmove, memset, memcmp, memchr
- strcpy, strncpy, strcat, strncat
- strlen, strcmp, strncmp
- strchr, strrchr, strstr
- sprintf, snprintf, sscanf

Fuzzing Strategy:
Structure-aware fuzzing with:
1. Variable source/dest buffer sizes
2. Overlapping regions
3. Null bytes in various positions
4. Maximum length strings
5. Misaligned pointers

Coverage Goals:
- All string function implementations in frankenlibc-core
- All membrane validation paths
- All healing action paths in hardened mode

Infrastructure:
- cargo-fuzz with libFuzzer
- Corpus seed with edge cases
- Nightly CI fuzzing (1M iterations)
- Crash reproduction scripts

Success Criteria:
- Zero crashes after 1B iterations
- All healing paths exercised
- Coverage report shows 80%+ of string code

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### RusticWolf Session 34 (2026-03-13) — Comprehensive String Fuzz Target

#### Deliverables
- **fuzz_targets/fuzz_string.rs**: Complete rewrite from stub to comprehensive structure-aware fuzz target
  - Uses `arbitrary` crate for structured fuzzing (not raw byte splitting)
  - 6 operation groups, 45 distinct test paths across all string/mem functions
  - Invariant assertions on every path (antisymmetry, self-compare, bound checks, etc.)
  - MAX_BUF=4096 clamp to prevent OOM while covering large-input paths

#### Coverage
- **Group 0 (mem ops)**: memcpy, memmove, memset, memcmp, memchr, memrchr, memmem, mempcpy, memccpy, bzero, bcmp, swab (12 sub-ops)
- **Group 1 (str cmp)**: strlen, strnlen, strcmp, strncmp, strcasecmp, strncasecmp, strcoll (7 sub-ops)
- **Group 2 (str copy)**: strcpy, stpcpy, strncpy, stpncpy, strcat, strncat, strlcpy, strlcat, strxfrm, strdup_bytes, strndup_bytes (10 sub-ops)
- **Group 3 (search)**: strchr, strchrnul, strrchr, strstr, strcasestr (5 sub-ops)
- **Group 4 (span/tok)**: strspn, strcspn, strpbrk, strsep, strtok, strtok_r (5 sub-ops)
- **Group 5 (misc)**: empty input handling, single-byte, cross-function consistency, memcpy/memmove agreement, strdup consistency, strlcpy round-trip (6 sub-ops)

#### Infrastructure
- Added `arbitrary = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }` dependency
- Added 7 seed corpus files in corpus/fuzz_string/ targeting edge cases: empty, short, uniform, maxn, binary, search, delim
- Fixed pre-existing clippy issue in fuzz_membrane.rs (manual_clamp)
- Fixed pre-existing fmt issue in fuzz_membrane.rs

#### Verification
- `cargo check`: PASS (via rch)
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`: PASS (via rch)
- `cargo fmt --check`: PASS (via rch)
- `cargo build --bin fuzz_string`: PASS (via rch)
- Workspace `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`: PASS (via rch)

#### Test Commands
```bash
# Build the fuzz target
cd crates/frankenlibc-fuzz && cargo build --bin fuzz_string

# Run with cargo-fuzz (requires nightly + -Zsanitizer=address)
cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_string -- -max_len=4096

# Run with seed corpus
cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_string corpus/fuzz_string/ -- -max_len=4096

# Run for N iterations
cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_string -- -max_len=4096 -runs=1000000
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:16.212096377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T15:57:28.331404449Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T15:57:28.330962803Z","close_reason":"Created fuzz harness for string ops","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzzing","string"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1oz.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1oz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1oz.2","title":"Fuzz target: allocator sequences","description":"Fuzz allocator for edge cases and corruption.

Target Functions:
- malloc, calloc, realloc, free
- posix_memalign, aligned_alloc, memalign
- malloc_usable_size

Fuzzing Strategy:
Sequence-based fuzzing:
1. Random allocation/free sequences
2. Varying sizes (0, 1, small, medium, large, huge)
3. Varying alignment requirements
4. Realloc size changes (grow, shrink)
5. Double-free attempts
6. Use-after-free attempts

Invariant Checks:
- Arena internal consistency
- Generation counter monotonicity
- Quarantine queue bounds
- Fingerprint integrity

Success Criteria:
- Zero crashes or hangs
- All allocator states reachable
- Quarantine prevents all UAF
- Double-free gracefully handled

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist

Expanded crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_malloc.rs beyond membrane-arena-only coverage: added core allocator state-machine operations for malloc/calloc/realloc/free, bogus free/realloc handling, lookup assertions, calloc-overflow exercise, and post-op active-count/live-pointer invariants while retaining existing arena/alignment coverage. Added allocator-sequence corpus seeds at crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/corpus/fuzz_malloc/seed_state_realloc_cycle and crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/corpus/fuzz_malloc/seed_state_calloc_overflow.

Validation (CPU-heavy via rch only):
- rch exec -- cargo check --manifest-path crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml : PASS

Notes:
- Initial rch validation attempt used package selection (-p frankenlibc-fuzz) and failed because cargo-fuzz workspace packaging does not expose that package ID in this workspace mode; reran with --manifest-path and validated successfully.
- I did not touch broader harness artifacts in this slice because the existing fuzz-harness test lane still has unrelated repo drift around fuzz_ctype conventions.","created_at":"2026-03-19T01:37:24Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-hxfu0","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] execvp_via_execve walks unbounded user-supplied `file` (bd-z4k96 class)","description":"FOUND + FIXED via review round 5 (commit 1d68447a).

execvp_via_execve at L62 dereferenced the caller's `file` pointer via CStr::from_ptr with no length bound. A non-NUL-terminated pathname (attacker-controlled or memory-corrupted) walked arbitrary process memory.

Reachability: every program that resolves a command via PATH (shell builtins, system(), subprocess wrappers, container runtime exec helpers) hits this path with caller-supplied text.

Fix: read_bounded_cstr replacement; on None return -1 + ENOENT.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commits 0c0b59f1 / 1d68447a / fe3f1c06; 3120/3120 abi tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bd-z4k96-class","high","review","unbounded-cstr"]}
 {"id":"bd-hzkc6","title":"syscall: signalfd4 existing fd update with same mask should return same fd without touching errno","description":"Host glibc returns the same fd and leaves errno unchanged for signalfd(existing_fd, &mask, 0). Add focused ABI regression coverage for the in-place update contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T10:56:03.717758642Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:06:51.635816783Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:06:51.633658460Z","close_reason":"signalfd4(existing_fd, same_mask, 0) → same_fd (in-place update) pinned.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-i18zl","title":"PORT: strvis + strnvis + strunvis + strnunvis (NetBSD vis(3) family)","description":"NetBSD vis(3) family — visual byte encoding/decoding used by mtree, fpr, cd9660, pkgsrc, BSD pkg tools and many ports for safely round-tripping arbitrary binary data through ASCII-only channels (file names in tar/cpio listings, log lines). Ship the four most commonly used variants: strvis(dst, src, flags) encodes src into dst (NUL terminated) returning the number of bytes written; strnvis(dst, dlen, src, flags) is the bounded variant returning -1 if dst too small; strunvis(dst, src) and strnunvis(dst, dlen, src) decode the inverse. Default mode encodes non-printable bytes as \\^X (control) or \\M-X (high-bit) per BSD vis(3); with VIS_OCTAL flag (0x01) all non-printable bytes go to \\NNN. Other VIS_* flags ignored in v1. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: pure-safe core::stdio::vis::{encode_byte, decode_one, strvis, strunvis} + abi shims that handle raw-pointer NUL and bounds.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:33:44.856900442Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:42:13.077049471Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T06:42:13.075032533Z","close_reason":"shipped: core::stdio::vis (encode_byte/decode_one + recursive M- handling) + stdio_abi strvis/strnvis/strunvis/strnunvis; 21+12 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-i3om4","title":"[HIGH] runtime_policy::ffi_pcc tests leak global state across parallel test threads","description":"## Symptom

`cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` fails with:

```
thread 'runtime_policy::tests::ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy' panicked at crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs:2168
test result: FAILED. 170 passed; 1 failed
```

Running the same test in isolation (`cargo test ... ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy -- --nocapture`) passes cleanly: '1 passed; 0 failed; 170 filtered out'.

## Diagnosis

The test already acquires `ffi_pcc_lock()` and calls `reset_ffi_pcc_state_for_tests`, `reset_decision_contract_machine_for_tests`, `enable_runtime_kernel_for_tests`, `set_mode_state_for_tests(MODE_HARDENED)`. That pattern suggests the author was aware of test-global-state coupling. The fact that the assertion at line 2168 (`assert_eq!(decision.policy_id, FFI_PCC_POLICY_BASE + 10)`) fires only under concurrent test scheduling means **at least one of those resets does not cover all mutable state that another test modifies concurrently** — either a static/lazy cache, a thread-local, or a singleton that is not inside the lock's scope.

Adjacent tests in the same module (`ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_snprintf` et al.) use the same lock and reset helpers and presumably race on the same shared state.

## Fix direction

1. Grep for every `static ATOMIC` / `LazyLock` / `thread_local` / `Mutex<` in runtime_policy.rs that the certificate gate path touches. Classify which are covered by `ffi_pcc_lock` vs `decision_contract_machine` vs neither.
2. Any state touched by `decide()` along the `lookup_active_ffi_pcc_certificate` -> `ffi_pcc_decision` path that is not inside one of the reset-helpers is a candidate leak.
3. Confirmed repro: run the full `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` twice in a row; the failure is sometimes the 'memcpy' test, sometimes 'snprintf' or another adjacent one — classic test-ordering-dependent leak.

## Blast radius

Blocks `cargo test --workspace`. Part of the same full-review batch as bd-g2bd5 (glibc_internal_abi_test heap corruption). Caught by cc-libc FULL REVIEW MODE.

## Verification

- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib runtime_policy::tests::ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy -- --nocapture` passes (1/1).
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` fails at the same test (170/171).

+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn __socketpair(
